description: Display metadata consumed by scripts/sync_paper_status.py. Paper-local status.json files remain the source of truth for proof status, review counts, and human summaries.

library_components.title: Foundations: finite math and graph tools
library_components.examples: Finite-sum rewrites, order/rank lemmas, threshold and interval characterizations, asymptotic/exponential estimates, and cycle extraction in finite directed graphs.

library_components.title: Foundations: probability and stochastic processes
library_components.examples: Finite distributions, conditional expectations, kernels, atom and variance lemmas, Gaussian and exponential calculations, stochastic dominance, Markov chains, MDPs, CTMCs, and renewal-reward identities.

library_components.title: Foundations: optimization and certificates
library_components.examples: Argmax and endpoint principles, finite-search certificates, approximation guarantees, linear-program certificates, binary-choice optimality, move-graph descent, and choice-equilibrium existence.

library_components.title: Rating-system applications
library_components.examples: Binary and ordinal signal models, posterior-mean ratings, bias/variance decompositions, prior-weighted updates, monotonicity/correction lemmas, and minimal bandit-regret interfaces.

library_components.title: Matching markets
library_components.examples: Preference profiles, blocking pairs and stable matchings, deferred-acceptance invariants, proposer incentives, and many-to-one admissions models.

library_components.title: Auctions and mechanisms
library_components.examples: Allocation and payment rules, dominant-strategy truthfulness, benchmark-competitive digital-goods auctions, VCG-style welfare maximization, and single-minded set-packing mechanisms.

library_components.title: Online algorithms and regret
library_components.examples: Online matching/allocation state machines, primal-dual accounting, competitive-ratio certificates, regret interfaces, and platform-learning abstractions.

library_components.title: Complexity abstractions
library_components.examples: Decision/search problem interfaces, reductions, NP/ZPP-style class consequences, Yao-style minimax wrappers, and explicit external-solver assumptions.

library_components.title: Applications: recommender systems
library_components.examples: Exposure and allocation policies, classwise fairness constraints, top-k recommendation surfaces, policy averaging, and accuracy/diversity trade-off statements.

library_components.title: Social choice, rankings, and fair division
library_components.examples: Ranking profiles, Kendall distance and Mallows-style sequential ranking models, score/payoff interfaces, envy graphs, bounded-envy allocations, and indivisible-goods fairness statements.
